articles.title: China’s Electrostate Is Poised to Win From War in the Middle East
articles.summary: A cluster of Chinese firms is poised to win big from the energy crisis set off by the war in the Middle East, building on booming investment in artificial intelligence.

articles.title: How a $75 billion windfall from Congress has insulated ICE
articles.summary: Last year, Congress approved $75 billion for immigration enforcement. That money has allowed ICE to operate nearly unfettered during a record-long shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.

articles.title: What a chimpanzee 'civil war' can teach us about how societies fall apart
articles.summary: A long-term study of the world's largest known community of chimpanzees has documented a rare event: what the researchers describe as the primate equivalent of a "civil war."

articles.title: 4 Takeaways From Orban’s Defeat in Hungary’s Election
articles.summary: Economics and unhappiness with endemic corruption helped Peter Magyar, the challenger, secure victory. A visit by the U.S. vice president in support of Mr. Orban didn’t change things.

articles.title: How Iran, Suffering Under Sanctions, Diversified Its Economy
articles.summary: As the nation contended with high inflation, high unemployment and unrest before the war, it became more than just an oil exporter.

articles.title: More than a quarter of private colleges are at risk of closing, a new projection shows
articles.summary: As one Vermont college finishes its last semester, an estimated 442 others may be in trouble.

articles.title: 2025 was one of most volatile years ever for U.S. naturalizations
articles.summary: The year began with many people becoming U.S. citizens, but by December, fewer people were doing so, driven by ramped-up scrutiny of applications and eroding trust in the system.

articles.title: The FAA takes a rare step to head off a traffic jam at Chicago's O'Hare Airport
articles.summary: Federal regulators want airlines to cut the number of flights at O'Hare Airport in Chicago this summer. It's an unusual move, sparked by a turf war between two major airlines with hubs at the airport.

articles.title: Can red light therapy really deliver a beauty and health glow up? Here's the science
articles.summary: Wellness influencers tout the therapy's power for everything from rejuvenating skin and hair to boosting longevity. Devices sold for at-home use abound. We look at the evidence behind the hype.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: President Trump says U.S. will blockade the Strait of Hormuz, failed U.S.-Iran negotiations cause oil prices to surge, Hungary's Viktor Orbán loses reelection bid.

articles.title: Gaza's ceasefire hasn't brought the surge of aid it promised
articles.summary: President Trump's ceasefire in Gaza was supposed to ease Israel's blockade, but restrictions on aid remain tight six months later.

articles.title: After Victor Orbán's election loss, what's next for Hungary?
articles.summary: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with David Pressman, who served as U.S. ambassador to Hungary in the Biden administration, about Victor Orban's election loss and what this means for Hungary.

articles.title: Strikes on alleged drug boats kill 5 in eastern Pacific, U.S. military says
articles.summary: The U.S. military said Sunday that it blew up two boats accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, as the Trump administration pursues its campaign against alleged traffickers in Latin A

articles.title: Rory McIlroy wins the Masters for second year in a row
articles.summary: Rory McIlroy goes back-to-back at the Masters to join Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo and Tiger Woods.

articles.title: A stampede at a Haitian mountaintop fortress kills at least 25 people and injures dozens
articles.summary: A stampede at a mountaintop fortress popular with tourists in northern Haiti has killed at least 25 people and injured dozens of others, authorities said.

articles.title: Peru election results delayed after thousands get a one-day voting extension
articles.summary: Voters will have to wait until at least Monday to learn the outcome of Sunday's presidential election after the process was mired with logistical issues that left thousands unable to cast ballots.

articles.title: Pope Leo says he does not fear Trump, as he pushes back in feud over Iran war
articles.summary: President Donald Trump delivered an extraordinary broadside against Pope Leo XIV on Sunday night, saying he didn't think the U.S.-born global leader of the Catholic Church is "doing a very good job."

articles.title: What to Know About Pope Leo’s Voyage to Africa
articles.summary: The pontiff will visit four countries on the world’s poorest continent, demonstrating his urgent focus on the most vulnerable members of the Church.

articles.title: Pope Leo Heads to Africa to Meet the Future of Catholicism
articles.summary: On a four-country tour of the continent, where the faith is growing faster than anywhere else, the pontiff will be greeted by enthusiastic crowds and authoritarian leaders.

articles.title: Orban Loss May Ease Hungary’s Tensions with European Union
articles.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has stymied the European Union for years, and particularly in recent months. Peter Magyar’s election could change that, if only so much.

articles.title: Who Is Peter Magyar, the Man Who Toppled Hungary’s Orban
articles.summary: Mr. Magyar’s success in the Hungarian election is fueled in large part by widespread public anger at corruption and concern about sluggish economic growth.

articles.title: Hungary Election Results: Viktor Orban Concedes Defeat and Congratulates Peter Magyar
articles.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has inspired populist movements globally, could not overcome the growing dissatisfaction of his own citizens.

articles.title: After failed peace talks, President Trump threatens to blockade the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Less than a day after peace talks between the US and Iran failed, President Trump said that he will blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
